reasoning: Based on comprehensive analysis, the probability of Fetterman ceasing to caucus with Democrats before July 1, 2025 is low at 18%. While May 2025 reporting reveals severe dysfunction - he removed himself from caucus group chats, skipped most hearings, faces staff concerns about mental health, and is described as 'isolated' - there are crucial factors keeping this probability below 20%: (1) his categorical denials in Jan 2025 that switching was 'not going to happen' and explicit statements he'd be a 'pretty bad Republican'; (2) the extremely tight 5-week timeline makes formal party switching or caucus removal unlikely without active ongoing processes; (3) he remains officially a Democrat (D-Pa) in all reporting; (4) no evidence of leadership actively seeking his removal or him planning a departure. The dysfunction indicates poor working relationships but doesn't necessarily lead to formal separation within this brief window.
